mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 24-month rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns exceeds +0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a mean forward return below zero during periods of concurrent RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals accelerating manufacturing output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45% across all expansion periods since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the rolling 36-month rank IC falls below -0.05 for more than 18 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in 65% of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4%, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP coinciding with falling real personal income signals a recessionary demand shock, which forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during contraction phases.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive during quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in a walk-forward test across all NBER-defined recessions.
